title: Tankers Research Update
summary:
Main
Muted VLCC response to OPEC+ accelerated July output hike
The eight members of OPEC+ agreed on May 31 to collectively raise crude production by 411k b/d in July, mirroring monthly increments approved for May and June. Speculation on the reasons behind the more rapid reversal of voluntary cuts by the eight continue to focus on a) bringing discipline to quota violators such as Kazakhstan and Iraq, b) reclaiming market share lost to US shale output and c) accommodating Washington's desire to lower global oil prices. OPEC+ members that continue to produce above their quotas include Kazakhstan (400k b/d above quota according to data from the OPEC Secretariat), UAE (estimated by the IEA to be overproducing by about 350k b/d) and Iraq (still exceeded its April target by 300k b/d).
